mission.title: cert-manager: How do I force a cert renewal after adding new entries to dnsNames?
mission.description: How do I force a cert renewal after adding new entries to dnsNames?. This issue affects 19+ users.

steps.title: Identify cert-manager troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cert-manager deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
I added a new entry in `Certificate`  `dnsNames` list
but after applying the new yaml, the status is still stuck in `Certificate scheduled for renewal in x hours`

Is there anyway I can force a renewal of current certificate?

steps.title: Apply the fix for How do I force a cert renewal after adding new entries to…
steps.description:
Ensures it's possible to update dnsNames on a certificate in order to trigger a re-issuer of a certificate.
```yaml
I tried using cert-manager command to refresh the cert, but I get:
```

steps.title: Confirm How do I force a cert renewal after adding new… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Ensures it's possible to update dnsNames on a certificate in order to trigger a re-issuer of a certificate.
codeSnippets: I tried using cert-manager command to refresh the cert, but I get:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
